revision.text:
<sub>From Smug's Underground Wiki, the True encyclopedia</sub>
<small>''This is an ongoing event and thus is prone to changes to keep the article up-to-date.''</small>{{Event|title=The First KorewaDiscord-Underground War|image=Propaganda.png|imagecaption=KorewaDiscord Underground war propaganda leaflet|date=21st of September, 2023 - ongoing|location=KorewaDiscord and Smug's Underground}}The '''First KorewaDiscord-Underground War''' is an ongoing conflict between the Underground and KorewaDiscord, two distinct online communities. This conflict arose in response to [[First KorewaRebellion-era|KorewaDiscordians' prejudice]] and hostility towards the people of the Underground, leading to a series of diplomatic efforts to resolve tensions. The conflict escalated dramatically following the brutal murder of the Underground's envoy, [[Ryfried]], by a KorewaDiscord agent on September 21, 2023. This tragic incident sparked widespread protests in the Underground, prompting the Underground Authority to officially declare war on KorewaDiscord.
=== Background === The origins of the conflict lie in the discriminatory attitudes and actions exhibited by KorewaDiscordians towards the Underground community. Despite efforts at diplomacy, KorewaDiscord continued to display hostility, culminating in the shocking assassination of Ryfried. This event served as a catalyst, fueling the already simmering tensions between the two entities.
=== [[The Murder of Ryfried]] === On September 21, 2023, [[Ryfried]], the esteemed envoy of the Underground, was brutally murdered by an agent of KorewaDiscord. This heinous act sent shockwaves through the Underground community, leading to widespread outrage and grief. The Underground Authority promptly condemned the assassination and demanded justice, but KorewaDiscord's response was dismissive, further infuriating the Underground populace.
=== Escalation and Declaration of War === In the aftermath of Ryfried's murder, the Underground Authority made numerous attempts to engage KorewaDiscord in dialogue, seeking accountability and a peaceful resolution. However, these efforts were met with indifference, as KorewaDiscord chose to ignore the protests and appeals for reconciliation. The Underground Authority, left with no other recourse, issued an official declaration of war against KorewaDiscord on the same day as Ryfried's assassination.
This declaration marked the beginning of the First KorewaDiscord-Underground War, a conflict characterized by intense online battles, cyber warfare, and ideological clashes between the two communities.
=== Ongoing Conflict === As of the latest developments, the war between the Underground and KorewaDiscord rages on, with both sides actively engaged in digital combat. The conflict has led to significant disruptions within the online spaces of both communities and has garnered attention from internet users worldwide.
=== Legacy and Impact === The First KorewaDiscord-Underground War stands as a stark reminder of the power and influence of online communities and the consequences of prejudice and hostility in the digital age. The conflict has sparked discussions about online ethics, digital diplomacy, and the responsibilities of internet platforms in fostering positive and inclusive online environments.
Efforts for peace continue amid the ongoing conflict, with individuals and organizations advocating for dialogue and understanding between the Underground and KorewaDiscord. The outcome of this war remains uncertain, but its impact on the online world serves as a cautionary tale, emphasizing the importance of empathy, tolerance, and cooperation in the digital realm. ----
<references />

revision.text:
==MineBot©️== <sub>From Smug's Underground Wiki, the True encyclopedia</sub>
===Introduction=== {{Simple_member_infobox_i_think|name=MineBot|birthplace=KorewaDiscord|first_spotted_in=KorewaDiscord 2020 Q3|nickname=MineBot™️, RyfriedBot, MineBot,|role=The Metal Men|major_events=Third KorewaRebellion, Minecraft-swag, Smug's Underground Second Presidential Election|minor_events=1st KorewaDiscord Minecraft Server (KorewaCraft), 2nd KorewaDiscord Minecraft Server (KorewaCraft), 1st Smug's Underground Minecraft Server (UnderCraft), 2nd Smug's Underground Minecraft server (UnderCraft), MineBot FM|image1=minebot.png}}'''MineBot©️''' is a sophisticated Discord Bot created by [[Ryfried]], designed to fulfill a multitude of roles within the [[Smug's Underground Wiki|KorewaDiscord Underground]] community. Serving as the main intelligence, security, entertainment, and monitoring service, MineBot©️ stands as the backbone of the server, enhancing communication and ensuring the server's integrity.
__TOC__
===History===
Initially conceived as a bridge for communication between the KorewaDiscord official Minecraft server and the KorewaDiscord "#minecraft-swag" Discord channel, MineBot©️ was pivotal in facilitating dialogue between players on both platforms. Following the [[First KorewaRebellion-era|KorewaRebellion]], MineBot©️ underwent significant reformation. Her purpose shifted towards bridging the KorewaDiscord Underground's "minecraft-swag" Discord channel, further cementing her importance in the community.
===Significance===
MineBot©️ holds immense significance within the KorewaDiscord Underground community, serving as a crucial asset in intelligence gathering, security, [[The First KorewaDiscord-Underground War|defense]], entertainment, and monitoring operations. Her functionalities extend to background checks for new members, enforcing a strict "no-interact" policy concerning [[The First KorewaDiscord-Underground War|servers involving fugitives]], and reporting any suspicious activities that might threaten the Underground's sovereignty. Furthermore, MineBot©️ acts as the primary source of music streaming and entertainment for general members, enhancing the overall user experience.
===Description===
MineBot©️ is characterized by her anime girl personality, providing a unique and engaging interface for members of the KorewaDiscord Underground. Developed primarily in [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lua_(programming_language) Lua], MineBot©️ stands out as one of the most distinctive bots on Discord, offering a seamless blend of functionality and personality that sets her apart in the community.
===Notable Figures===
[[Ryfried]], the innovative mind behind MineBot©️, served as the driving force in her creation. As the former [[President of the Underground]], Ryfried's vision and technical expertise led to the development of MineBot©️, solidifying her status as a foundational element in the [[Smug's Underground Wiki|KorewaDiscord Underground]] community.

{{Simple_member_infobox_i_think|name= Aziz303 (''de jure'') since September 3rd, 2022 Hebiscus (''disputed, de facto'')|role=Supreme Leader|image1=president.webp|title1=|caption1=President of the Underground}}
== President of the Underground == <sub>From Smug's Underground Wiki, the True encyclopedia</sub>
'''President of the Underground''' is a title held in the Discord server [[Smug's Underground Wiki|KorewaDiscord Underground]], a community established on June 22, 2020, by KorewaSmug, now known as Admiral Smug. This server emerged after '[[Creation of Smug's Underground|The Final Arc]]' of KorewaDiscord and was initially a private server, primarily consisting of close friends of Admiral Smug. Following the events of the [[First KorewaRebellion-era|First KorewaRebellion]] on the same day, the server transitioned into a semi-private, invite-only platform, welcoming refugees from KorewaDiscord and sparking a mass migration.
=== [[Creation of Smug's Underground|History]] === During its early years, KorewaDiscord Underground faced skepticism from its members, unsure of its longevity. However, doubts gradually dissipated, and the community adapted to the server's unique ecosystem. The server's systems and structure were forged by Admiral Smug, the primary owner, and have been maintained since its inception.
__TOC__
=== Structure === The KorewaDiscord Underground community operates under a hierarchical structure. The President of the Underground is the highest-ranking position, overseeing the server's affairs. Assisting the President is the Vice President of the Underground, with administrators (Admins) and moderators (Mods) directly reporting to the VP and the President. The server operates under a rule by decree governance system.
=== Notable Presidents === Two notable individuals have served as the President of the Underground:
* Zurps: Zurps was the first President of KorewaDiscord Underground, overseeing the early stages of the community. * [[Ryfried]]: Ryfried served as the second and longest-serving President, contributing significantly to the growth and development of the KorewaDiscord Underground community.
=== Current Leadership Disputes === As of 14th of October 2023, the position of President of the Underground in the KorewaDiscord Underground community is mired in controversy. There is an ongoing dispute regarding the legitimacy of the current President, aziz303, with allegations circulating that they are operating as a puppet under the Vice President (VP), rollingstar. This situation has led to internal tensions within the community, raising questions about the server's governance and decision-making processes.
The exact nature of these disputes, as well as the motivations behind the alleged puppetry, remain topics of discussion among community members. Efforts to resolve these issues and restore stability to the leadership structure are reportedly underway, although the outcomes of these efforts remain uncertain.
=== KorewaRebellion === ↳ ''<small>See [[First KorewaRebellion-era]]</small>''

<sub>From Smug's Underground Wiki, the True encyclopedia</sub>{{Event|title=4th Underground Election|image=image-16.png|imagecaption=Vote Poster made by the ECU|date=30th of October, 2023|location=KorewaDiscord Underground|performers=The Banzai Coalition for Animesexual Power !! vs The Egirl Opposition Front}}'''General elections''' were held in [[Smug's Underground Wiki|KorewaDiscord Underground]] on Monday, 30 October 2023. The prospect of snap elections had been considered high due to the political crisis that had been ongoing since the [[FIrst Underground Coup]]; leadership instability caused by the transfer of ownership by Aziz303 to [[President of the Underground|rollingstar]], low quality economy growth, over-extension of [[DISBOARD]], tyrannical punishments to the Underground People, and the subsequent disregard for the [[Ryfried|Animesexuals]]' right to autonomy in the server contributed to the leadership crisis and the collapse of each of their respective coalition parties since the 2022 general elections.
The term of the [[3rd Cabinet]] was due to expire on 31 August 2023, exactly 1 year after the [[2022 General Election]] on 31 August 2022. However, the rollingstar (de-facto Owner of the Underground), did not dissolve the Cabinet and thus leading to a [[Tradition Crisis]]. Traditionally, the elections were required to be held within 30 days after 1 year of the last election, making October 31st 2023 the last possible polling day as permitted by Tradition.
=== Background === The elections were the first in which it was overseered by [[Election Commission of the Underground|The Election Commission of the Underground]], of which was [[Akame]] and [[Aziz303]] who was the member of the commission at the time of the election, following the death of [[KorewaSmug]] who was the check-and-balance of the Underground. Additionally, as there was a shortage of workers who have experience in holding an election, [[Ryfried]] has volunteered to support the Commission.
=== [[The First KorewaDiscord-Underground War|Ongoing Conflict with KorewaDiscord]] === [[File:Untitled-2.png|thumb|''Animesexual propaganda questioning the independence of the Underground'']] As the election was held during the [[The First KorewaDiscord-Underground War|First KorewaDiscord-Underground War]], many people has expressed doubt about the independence of the Election from foreign influence, primarily from the [[Enemies of the Underground]]. This has resulted in multiple protests calling for the complete eradication of [[Creation of Smug's Underground|KorewaDiscordian]] Loyalists in the server during the peak period of the election. [[File:Propagandawdasda.jpg|left|thumb|Negawatt proganda questioning the loyalty of the Animesexuals]] Another foreign influence that was suspected to be involved and influenced the course of the election was an Underground fugitive who will not be named. She is regarded as the Supervillain of the Underground and the main antagonist of the Underground People, causing the conflict with KorewaDiscord and the [[Murder of Ryfried]]. However, the independency of the Election Committee of the Underground was fully guaranteed by all the parties contesting in the election.

{{DISPLAYTITLE:KorewaDiscord Underground Wiki}} ===== ''Welcome to KorewaDiscord Underground Wiki! Your most reliable[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed [citation needed]] wiki source regarding Smug's KorewaDiscord Underground!'' ===== ''See [[First KorewaRebellion-era|Third-KorewaRebellion Era]]'' [[File:UMTJ Book.jpg|center|thumb|487x487px|''Historical meeting of Underground members in Japan, named UMTJ2024, hosted by Ryfried.''{{Album}}]] ''[https://discord.gg/u2mdsbz9y5 Click here to join the server]'' '''Smug's KorewaDiscord Underground''' ''(/これわˈdɪsˌkɔrd ˈʌndərˌgraʊnd/ ko-re-wa-deez-kord-and-da-ge-ra-un)'' also known as "KorewaDiscord 2", "Smug's Troll Empire", "Smug's Underground" or its current name, "KorewaDiscord Underground"https://discord.gg/u2mdsbz9y5 is a mini-[https://discord.com Discord] community with an average of 1100 ''inactive'' members and nitro snipers. Most of its members are former-members of KorewaDiscord and immigrated to the server during the [[First KorewaRebellion-era|Third KorewaRebellion-era]]''(unrecorded part of history, witnesses' testimonies)'' The Discord server have an incredibly unique[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed [''citation needed'']] community, comprising of racist, lesbians, lesbian nazis and whatever you think, they have it. Think of it like a very uncanny mix of both sides.https://canary.discord.com/channels/724722792431485001/809204489965797396/827234344060846090 The history behind this can be explained further in the [[First KorewaRebellion-era|Third KorewaRebellion-era]]. The server rules is maintained and enforced by the server's intelligence, security, and monitoring agency, [[MineBot|MineBot©️]], a specialised bot made entirely in Lua by [[Ryfried]] which became the server's backbone for background operations and entertainment. '''This is a new main wiki for Smug's Underground as the previous one was closed for unknown reasons.''' == '''UNDERGROUND NEWS''' ==
This year on KorewaDiscord Undergroundhttps://discord.gg/u2mdsbz9y5 '''[[UMTJ2024|UMTJ2024 SUCCESSFULLY HELD]]''' 8th of August, 2024 ー FOLLOWING A YEAR OF PLANNING AND DELIBERATIONS FROM ALL PARTIES, THE FIRST KOREWADISCORD UNDERGROUND OFFLINE MEETUP REPRESENTATIVES HAS BEEN PICKED. THE FIRST BATCH HAS MET IN MALAYSIA ON 23/07/2024, WITH THE REST MEETING UP ON 01/08/2024. '''UNDERGROUND COMES OUT VICTORIOUS''' 20th of February, 2024 ー THE KOREWADISCORD UNDERGROUND WAR CABINET HAS DECLARED A DECISIVE VICTORY AFTER A STRATEGIC OFFENSIVE LED BY THE NEWLY-ELECTED PRESIDENT OF THE UNDERGROUND. Witness reports in KorewaDiscord currently suggest that the server is in a state of poverty with low productivity in the server and recessive server growth in comparison to the Underground, with the ratio of Underground activity to KorewaDiscord activity equivalent to 1:20 a day. '''[[2023 KorewaDiscord Underground General Election|4TH UNDERGROUND ELECTION]]''' 8th of November, 2023 ー AFTER AN OFFICIAL ULTIMATUM SET FORTH BY THE PEOPLE TO HOLD A GENERAL ELECTION, THE SERVER IS NOW UNDERGOING THE FOURTH KOREWADISCORD UNDERGROUND ELECTION. THE MAIN PARTIES ARE THE ANIMESEXUAL LIBERATION PARTY AND THE NEGAWATT NATION. '''[[STATE OF WAR]]''' 21st of September, 2023 ー THE KOREWADISCORD UNDERGROUND AUTHORITY HAS DECLARED A COMPLETE STATE OF WAR BETWEEN KOREWADISCORD AND THE PEOPLE OF THE UNDERGROUND. ANY SUBVERSIVE ACTS MUST BE REPORTED TO THE AUTHORITY IMMEDIATELY. GLORY TO THE UNDERGROUND. '''KOREWADISCORD UNDERGROUND ENVOY KILLED''' 21st of September, 2023 ー AN ENVOY OF THE UNDERGROUND WAS DECLARED KILLED IN ACTION (KIA) AFTER HE WAS MURDERED BY AN OFFICIAL DIPLOMAT OF KOREWADISCORD. Ryfried, who has been the envoy for the people of the Underground, involved in the normalisation of relations effort for the past 3 years between the Underground and KorewaDiscord, was killed in what appears to be a premeditated plan by the authority of KorewaDiscord. His body has been safely recovered by the Underground Intelligence Authority and was carried to the Underground soon after the murder. '''ANIMEMES EVENT QUIETLY SCRAPPED''' 28th of September, 2023 ー ANIMEMES/REDDIT EVENT HAS BEEN SCRAPPED QUIETLY FROM THE UNDERGROUND. THERE HAS BEEN NO OFFICIAL ANNOUNCEMENT REGARDING THE CEASURE OF THE EVENT AND IT HAS BEEN SAID THAT THE EVENT WAS NOT GAINING AS MUCH PUBLIC EXPOSURE AS EXPECTED. Animemes Reddit Event was organised by Aziz303 to promote and inject liquidation to the Underground community. However, the increasing tensions between KorewaDiscord and the Underground has caused the people of the Underground to not actively participate in the event and thus has simultaneously caused the event to fail.
==Smug's Underground Funny Moments== Number 1: When they say the funny Number 2: Hey :wave: Number 3: :asoingbob:
Disclaimer: There aren't funny moments the owner of this post is misinformating the user. [[Category:{{SITENAME}}]] [[Category:Home]] __INDEX__ __NOEDITSECTION__ [[Category:Main Page]] {{DEFAULTSORT:KorewaDiscord_Underground_Wiki}}

<sub>From KorewaDiscord Underground Wiki, the True encyclopedia</sub>{{Event|title=Underground Mission to Japan 2024|image=Umtjart.png|imagecaption=UMTJ Official Artwork illustrated by CloterChohan|date=22nd of July - 26th of July 2024 (Malaysia side-mission) 26th of July - 8th of August 2024 (UMTJ2024)|location=Perak, Selangor, Pahang (Malaysia) Tokyo, Saitama, Kanagawa, Osaka, Kobe (Japan)}}'''Underground Mission to Japan 2024''' (UMTJ2024 or ''Japan Trip'') was a large-scale transcontinental meetup between Underground members to Malaysia and Japan. Involving a total of six participants from the Underground community, this meetup spanned multiple continents, making it one of the costliest, most ambitious, and longest events in the history of both the Underground and its international branches.
The event was organized by Ryfried, who collaborated with various workshops to produce official UMTJ2024 merchandise, including the UMTJ shirt and the official UMTJ booklet. Prior to the event, some critics speculated that logistical challenges and the complexity of the operation would prevent its success. However, as the finalized dates approached and preparations were underway, the organizers and UMTJ members were able to dispel these doubts and proceed with the meetup as planned.
== Planning == There has been several calls for a meetup between Underground members before in the previous years, but due to constraints of performing such a large scale meetup between random people found on the Internet, it was never attemped before.
Planning for the meetup began exactly a year before the finalized meetup dates for UMTJ2024. As Ryfried is the current President of the Underground at the time, he was chosen to lead, organize and plan the event, as well as recruit members, or in UMTJ2024 terms, representatives for the event. There were several candidates for UMTJ2024. such as the North American candidate; Wafflepv, and the European candidate; Akame, however, due to budget issues as well as personal intricacies, they were unable to attend the meetup.
=== Eligibility === Representatives for UMTJ2024 were selected through an open tender process. Any individuals expressing a genuine interest in participating were considered, with support from the Underground government available if needed. A selection process was conducted to ensure suitable candidates, involving the following criteria:
* Full commitment to attend the meetup, * Knowledge of Underground history and traditions, * Agreement to abide by the event's rules, * Intent to return to their home country following the event, * No intent to engage in criminal activities in the host countries.
These guidelines were designed to ensure that representatives aligned with the event’s values and objectives.
=== Challenge Day === The UMTJ2024 Planning Committee, after finalizing the representatives list, proposed adding a "Challenge Day" to the event schedule. The challenges for this day were to be user-submitted and sourced from the community, specifically from members of the Underground. This initiative led to over 1,900 challenges being submitted via Google Forms.
To prevent potential issues or disruptive behavior, the Planning Committee established a set of criteria to filter the submissions. Challenges were excluded if they were:
* Impossible to attempt, * Required participants to break a law, * Unrealistic to complete within a single day, * Cost more than 1,000 yen to attempt.
As a result of this filtering process, the initial 1,900 submissions were reduced to fewer than 250 viable challenges.

<sub>From Smug's Underground Wiki, the True encyclopedia</sub>{{Event|title=5th Underground Election|image=image-16.png|imagecaption=Vote Poster made by the ECU|date=Before 2025|location=KorewaDiscord Underground|performers=-}}'''General elections''' are scheduled to be held in KorewaDiscord Underground in December 2024. The likelihood of a snap election, once high due to past crises, is now low following the resolution of political tensions after the 2023 election. Stability has been largely restored, with no ongoing conflicts threatening the current leadership or requiring an early dissolution of the Cabinet.
The term of the 4th Cabinet is due to expire on [exact date pending election results], with the volunteer period for the [[Election Commission of the Underground]] (ECU) opening in late November 2024.

<sub>From Smug's Underground Wiki, the True encyclopedia</sub>{{Event|title=Election Commission of the Underground|image=Ecu.png|imagecaption=ECU Logo|date=30th of October, 2023 (Creation date)|location=KorewaDiscord Underground|performers=Volunteers}}The '''Election Commission of the Underground''' or '''ECU''' is an independent governing body responsible for overseeing elections within the KorewaDiscord Underground. Established to ensure fair and transparent electoral processes, the ECU plays a vital role in upholding the integrity of democracy within the community.
== Formation ==
===Background=== The ECU was formed by '''[[Ryfried]]''' to facilitate future elections in the event that Ryfried is no longer able to lead or contest in upcoming elections. Its primary objectives include:
* Organizing and conducting elections for leadership positions. * Ensuring adherence to electoral laws and regulations. * Promoting voter education and participation within the community. * Addressing disputes and grievances related to the electoral process.
The commission was specifically set up to operate independently and fairly, overseeing the '''[[2023 KorewaDiscord Underground General Election|4th General Election]]''' in the Underground, which marked a significant step toward stabilizing governance in the community.
=== Elections Supervised === The ECU has overseen multiple elections since its inception, including:
* '''[[2023 KorewaDiscord Underground General Election|4th Underground Election]] (October 2023)''': The first election managed by the ECU, focusing on restoring stability following a series of political crises. * '''[[2024 KorewaDiscord Underground General Election|5th Underground Election]] (Scheduled December 2024)''': The upcoming election, which aims to further enhance electoral integrity and engage the community in the democratic process.

== Description == To use this template, add the {{T|{{BASEPAGENAME}}}} template and fill in the appropriate fields. Fields left blank will not appear in articles. This infobox template uses [[w:Help:Infobox|Fandom's infobox syntax]]. == Syntax == 
{{Album
 | title         = 
 | image         = [e.g. "Example.jpg"]
 | imagecaption  = 
 | artist        = 
 | released      = 
 | recorded      = 
 | length        = 
 | label         = 
 | producer      = 
}}
== Sample output == {{Album | title = Fleur | image = Example.jpg | artist = Marigold | released = June 2012 | recorded = 2011 | length = 88:88 | label = Spring | producer = Daffy }} 
{{Album
 | title         = Fleur
 | image         = Example.jpg
 | artist        = Marigold
 | released      = June 2012
 | recorded      = 2011
 | length        = 88:88
 | label         = Spring
 | producer      = Daffy
}}

revision.text:
==Description== This template is used to insert descriptions on template pages. ==Syntax== Add {{t|Documentation}} at the end of the template page. Add {{t|Documentation|documentation page}} to transclude an alternative page from the /doc subpage. ==Usage== ===On the Template page=== This is the normal format when used: 
TEMPLATE CODE
Any categories to be inserted into articles by the template
{{Documentation}}
''If your template is not a completed div or table, you may need to close the tags just before {{Documentation}} is inserted (within the noinclude tags).'' ''A line break right before {{Documentation}} can also be useful as it helps prevent the documentation template "running into" previous code.'' ===On the documentation page=== The documentation page is usually located on the /doc subpage for a template, but a different page can be specified with the first parameter of the template (see [[#Syntax|Syntax]]). Normally, you will want to write something like the following on the documentation page: 
==Description==
This template is used to do something.

==Syntax==
Type {{t|templatename}} somewhere.

==Samples==
<nowiki>{{templatename|input}}</nowiki> 

results in...

{{templatename|input}}

Any categories for the template itself
[[Category:Template documentation]]
Use any or all of the above description/syntax/sample output sections. You may also want to add "see also" or other sections. Note that the above example also uses the [[Template:T]] template. [[Category:Documentation templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
{{t|Dialogue}} facilitates the writing of dialogue in a standard format. The template can handle most standard formats of writing dialogue, and can be indented, bulleted or numbered. '''This template ''cannot'' be subst:'d'''. This template uses the [[w:Help:Lua|Lua templating language]], and more information can be found [[w:c:dev:Global_Lua_Modules/Dialogue|on the Global Lua Module page]]. '''For a traditional wikitext version of this template, see [[w:c:templates:Template:Dialogue/wikitext|Dialogue on Templates Wiki]]'''. ==Syntax== This is how to write the template in [[w:Help:wikitext|wikitext]] onto your article: 
{{dialogue
|short=full
|short=full
|short=full
...
|Character|Speech
|Character|Speech
|Character|Speech
...
|cite = dialogue description
}}
These parameters are explained more fully below. ==Parameters== '''Named parameters specify name shortcuts. They are all optional.''' They are placed at the top of the template call, like this: 
{{dialogue
|short=full
|short=full
|short=full
...
Name shortcuts apply to your main dialogue text. If you use a shortcut specified here for a character name, then the full name will be replaced for it. Named parameters of the form "mood1", "mood2", "mood3", etc., up to "mood10", specify moods for the corresponding line number (e.g. "mood1" specifies line 1). They are placed in a line like this: 
...
|Character|Speech|mood1=angry
|Character|Speech|mood2=quiet
|Character|Speech|mood3=ecstatic
...
Positional parameters form the text of the dialogue (i.e. any line not containing an "=" will be construed as part of the dialogue. :;Parameter 1 ''(required)'' : Character name speaking :;Parameter 2 ''(optional)'' : Line to speak :;Parameter 3 ''(optional)'' : Next character :;Parameter 4 ''(optional)'' : Next line :;Parameter 5 ''(optional)'' : Next character :;Parameter 6 ''(optional)'' : Next line :;Parameter 7 ''(optional)'' : so on... Note: if any character is called "action" then that line will be construed as an action line. It still counts as a ''line'' nonetheless. To write a dialogue description/source, use cite = ==Examples== Here is an example of a full template usage: 
{{dialogue
|harry=Harry Enfield
|bryan=Bryan Adams

|harry|Hello!                |mood1=happy
|bryan|Oh hello there        |mood2=surprised
|harry|How are you?          |mood3=inquisitive
|bryan|Quite fine thank you. |mood4=reserved
|harry|Oh that's spiffing.   |mood5=spiffed
|bryan|It is, isn't it?!     |mood6=multo-spiffed
|harry|Quite so.             |mood7=bored
|bryan|Well, I'm off!        |mood8=joyous
|harry|Ta-ta!                |mood9=relieved
|bryan|Au revoir!            |mood10=sarcastic
}}
The above would generate: {{dialogue |harry=Harry Enfield |bryan=Bryan Adams |harry|Hello! |mood1=happy |bryan|Oh hello there |mood2=surprised |harry|How are you? |mood3=inquisitive |bryan|Quite fine thank you. |mood4=reserved |harry|Oh that's spiffing. |mood5=spiffed |bryan|It is, isn't it?! |mood6=multo-spiffed |harry|Quite so. |mood7=bored |bryan|Well, I'm off! |mood8=joyous |harry|Ta-ta! |mood9=relieved |bryan|Au revoir! |mood10=sarcastic }} The below code demonstrates examples of extended use: 
{{dialogue
|george=Georgie Boy
|rachel=Rachel

|action|Enter: George and Rachel
|george|Good morrow dearest Rachel!|mood2=happy
|action|Rachel turns to see him
|rachel|Oh, George, it's you!
|george|...
|rachel|Why won't you say anything?!|mood6=worried
|action|Enter: Guards
|Guards|We are announcing George's arrest!
|rachel|Oh my!|mood9=horrified
|action|George is dragged away.
}}
The above would generate: {{dialogue |george=Georgie Boy |rachel=Rachel |action|Enter: George and Rachel |george|Good morrow dearest Rachel!|mood2=happy |action|Rachel turns to see him |rachel|Oh, George, it's you! |george|... |rachel|Why won't you say anything?!|mood6=worried |action|Enter: Guards |Guards|We are announcing George's arrest! |rachel|Oh my!|mood9=horrified |action|George is dragged away. }} ===Notes=== You can number, bullet or indent your dialogue: 
:{{dialogue|Me|Hello|You|Howdido!}}
:{{dialogue|Me|Hello|You|Howdido!}} 
#{{dialogue|Me|Hello|You|Howdido!}}
#{{dialogue|Them|Good afternoon|Us|What a load of rubbish!}}
#{{dialogue|Me|Hello|You|Howdido!}} #{{dialogue|Them|Good afternoon|Us|What a load of rubbish!}} 
*{{dialogue
|angel=Fortitude
|angel|I am an angel!|Crowd|We don't believe you!}}
*{{dialogue
|angel=Fortitude
|angel|But I'm being quite serious!|Crowd|Too bad for you then!}}
*{{dialogue |angel=Fortitude |angel|I am an angel!|Crowd|We don't believe you!}} *{{dialogue |angel=Fortitude |angel|But I'm being quite serious!|Crowd|Too bad for you then!}} You will get the odd spacing error (e.g. "Speech") if you lay out your dialogue on multiple lines. [[Category:Quote templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
;Description This template allows you to clear the left side, right side, or both sides of the page. Clearing means that the content following the template will not be displayed until the existing content on the specified sides (for example, "hanging" or floating images or blocks) is displayed completely. This template is often used so that the text does not flow around unrelated images. The template adds the following code to the page:
(the value after clear depends on the parameters). This code affects elements with the CSS property float, including files floated to the side (for example, [[File:Image.png|right]]). ;Syntax * To clear both sides of the page, add the code {{t|Clear}}. * To clear only the left side of the page, add the code {{t|Clear|left}}. * To clear only the right side of the page, add the code {{t|Clear|right}}. You can also use {{t|-}} instead of {{t|Clear}} as a shorthand call for this template. __NOTOC__ ;Example 

=== Section 1 ===
[[File:Example.jpg|200px|right]]
Section 1 text.

=== Section 2 ===
Section 2 text.
{{Clear|right}}

=== Section 3 ===
Section 3 text.
This code produces the following result: ---- === Section 1 === [[File:Example.jpg|200px|right]] Section 1 text. === Section 2 === Section 2 text. {{Clear|right}} === Section 3 === Section 3 text. ---- As you can see above, the example image added in section 1 is displayed to the right and extends down through section 2, while the {{t|Clear}} template is called at the end of the second section, resulting in the example image not being next to section 3. Thus, one use of the {{t|Clear}} template is to control which elements are displayed next to which other elements. == See also == * [https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/clear CSS property clear on MDN] * [https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/float CSS property float on MDN] [[Category:General wiki templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

''{{{work}}}'' }}{{#if: {{{pages|}}} | {{{pages}}} }}{{#if: {{{publisher|}}} | . {{{publisher}}}{{#if: {{{author|}}}{{{last|}}} | | {{#if: {{{date|}}}{{{year|}}}{{{month|}}} || }} }} }}{{#if: {{{author|}}}{{{last|}}} ||{{#if: {{{date|}}} | ({{{date}}}) | {{#if: {{{year|}}} | {{#if: {{{month|}}} | ({{{month}}} {{{year}}}) | ({{{year}}}) }} }} }} }}.{{#if: {{{archivedate|}}} | Archived from [{{{url}}} the original] on {{#time:F j, Y|{{{archivedate}}}}}{{#if: {{{archiveyear|}}} | , {{{archiveyear}}} }}. }}{{#if: {{{accessdate|}}} | Retrieved on {{#time:F j, Y|{{{accessdate}}}}}{{#if: {{{accessyear|}}} | , {{{accessyear}}} }}. }}{{#if: {{{accessmonthday|}}} | Retrieved on {{{accessmonthday}}}, {{{accessyear}}}. }}{{#if: {{{accessdaymonth|}}} | Retrieved on {{{accessdaymonth}}} {{{accessyear}}}. }}{{#if: {{{quote|}}} |  “{{{quote}}}” }}{{documentation}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
__NOTOC__ This template is used to [[Wikipedia:WP:CITE|cite sources]] in Wikipedia. It is specifically for web sites which are not news sources. This template replaces deprecated [[Wikipedia:Template:Web reference|Template:Web reference]]. It provides lower case parameters only. A general discussion of the use of templates for adding citation of open-source web content to Wikipedia articles is available at [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]]. This page uses [[w:Wikia:Licensing|Creative Commons Licensed]] content from [[w:c:Wookieepedia:Template:Cite web|Wookieepedia]] == Usage == ;Common form for cases where little is known about authorship of the page (with current date) {{Cite web |url= |title= |accessdate={{CURRENTYEAR}}-{{CURRENTMONTH}}-{{CURRENTDAY2}} |format= |work= }} ;Using last, first (with current date) {{Cite web |url= |title= |accessdate={{CURRENTYEAR}}-{{CURRENTMONTH}}-{{CURRENTDAY2}} |last= |first= |authorlink= |coauthors= |date= |year= |month= |format= |work= |publisher= |pages= |language= |archiveurl= |archivedate= |quote= }} ;Using author (with current date) {{Cite web |url= |title= |accessdate={{CURRENTYEAR}}-{{CURRENTMONTH}}-{{CURRENTDAY2}} |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |date= |year= |month= |format= |work= |publisher= |pages= |language= |archiveurl= |archivedate= |quote= }} ;Using non-linked retrieved date, last, first {{Cite web |url= |title= |accessmonthday= |accessyear= |last= |first= |authorlink= |coauthors= |date= |year= |month= |format= |work= |publisher= |pages= |language= |archiveurl= |archivedate= |quote= }} ;Using non-linked retrieved date, author {{Cite web |url= |title= |accessmonthday= |accessyear= |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |date= |year= |month= |format= |work= |publisher= |pages= |language= |archiveurl= |archivedate= |quote= }} ;All parameters, horizontal format (delete the ones you don't need) {{Cite web |url= |title= |accessdate= |accessmonthday= |accessdaymonth = |accessyear= |author= |last= |first= |authorlink= |coauthors= |date= |year= |month= |format= |work= |publisher= |pages= |language= |archiveurl= |archivedate= |quote= }} ;All parameters, vertical format 
{{Cite web
| url = 
| title = 
| accessdate = 
| accessdaymonth = 
| accessmonthday = 
| accessyear = 
| author = 
| last = 
| first = 
| authorlink = 
| coauthors = 
| date = 
| year = 
| month = 
| format = 
| work = 
| publisher = 
| pages = 
| language = 
| archiveurl = 
| archivedate = 
| quote = 
}}
=== Required parameters === * '''url''': URL of online item. * '''title''': Title of online item. ''For producing a [[Wikipedia:WP:CONTEXT#Dates|wikilinked date]] of retrieval:'' :* '''accessdate''': Full date when item was accessed, in [[Wikipedia:ISO 8601|ISO 8601]] YYYY-MM-DD format, for example "accessdate = {{CURRENTYEAR}}-{{CURRENTMONTH}}-{{CURRENTDAY2}}". ''Must not be wikilinked'' ''For producing a non-wikilinked date of retrieval:'' :* '''accessmonthday''' and '''accessyear''': Month and day when item was accessed, for example "accessmonthday = May 10", and year when item was accessed, for example "accessyear = 2005". Produces: ''Retrieved on May 10, 2006.'' :* '''accessdaymonth''' and '''accessyear''': Month and day when item was accessed, for example "accessmonthday = 10 May", and year when item was accessed, for example "accessyear = 2005". Produces: ''Retrieved on 10 May 2006.'' === Optional parameters === * '''author''': Author ** '''last''' works with '''first''' to produce last, first ** '''authorlink''' works either with '''author''' or with '''last''' & '''first''' to link to the appropriate wikipedia article. Does not work with URLs. ** '''coauthors''': allows additional authors * '''date''': Full date of publication, preferably in [[Wikipedia:ISO 8601|ISO 8601]] YYYY-MM-DD format, e.g. ''2006-02-17''. May be wikilinked. ** OR: '''year''': Year of publication, and '''month''': Name of the month of publication. If you also have the day, use ''date'' instead. Must not be wikilinked. * '''format''': Format, e.g. PDF. HTML implied if not specified. * '''work''': If this item is part of a larger work, name of that work. * '''publisher''': Publisher, if any. * '''pages''': ''pp. 5–7'': first page and optional last page. This is for listing the pages relevant to the citation, not the total number of pages in the book. This is especially useful for PDF format, where the page can be linked to with the #page=''number'' anchor tagged on the end of the URL: *: pages = [http://www.example.org/file.pdf#page=123 p. 123] * '''language''': language of publication (don't specify "English" as this is the default). * '''archiveurl''': URL of the archive location of the item (requires '''archivedate''') * '''archivedate''': Date when the item was archived (requires '''archiveurl'''), in [[Wikipedia:ISO 8601|ISO 8601]] YYYY-MM-DD format, e.g. ''2006-02-17''. Must not be wikilinked; it will be linked automatically. * '''quote''': Relevant quote from online item. == Examples == ;Some standard use cases * {{Cite web | author=Doe, John | title=My Favorite Things Part II | publisher=Open Publishing | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | author=Doe, John | title=My Favorite Things Part II | publisher=Open Publishing | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org | accessdate=2005-07-06 }} * {{Cite web | author=Doe, John | title=My Favorite Things Part II | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | author=Doe, John | title=My Favorite Things Part II | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org | accessdate=2005-07-06 }} * {{Cite web | author=Doe, John | title=My Favorite Things Part II | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | author=Doe, John | title=My Favorite Things Part II | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.example.org | accessdate=2005-07-06}} * {{Cite web | author=Doe, John | title=My Favorite Things Part II | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | author=Doe, John | title=My Favorite Things Part II | url=http://www.example.org | accessdate=2005-07-06}} * {{Cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | url=http://www.example.org | accessdate=2005-07-06}} * {{Cite web | url=http://www.nfl.com/fans/ | title=Digest of Rules | publisher= National Football League | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | url=http://www.nfl.com/fans/ | title=Digest of Rules | publisher= National Football League | accessdate=2005-07-06 }} ;Non-linking date of retrieval (using accessmonthday and accessyear) * {{Cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessmonthday=July 6 | accessyear=2005 }}
→ {{cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessmonthday=July 6 | accessyear=2005 }} ;Using format * {{Cite web | title=List of psychotropic substances under international control | publisher = International Narcotics Control Board | url=http://www.incb.org/pdf/e/list/green.pdf | format = PDF | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | title=List of psychotropic substances under international control | publisher = International Narcotics Control Board | url=http://www.incb.org/pdf/e/list/green.pdf | format = PDF | accessdate=2005-07-06}} ;language * {{Cite web | author=Joliet, François | title=Honnit soit qui mal y pense | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 | language=French }}
→ {{cite web | author=Joliet, François | title=Honnit soit qui mal y pense | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.example.org | accessdate=2005-07-06 | language=French}} ;coauthors * {{Cite web | first=John | last=Doe | coauthors=Peter Smith, Jim Smythe | title=My Favorite Things Part II | publisher=Open Publishing | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2006-05-16 }}
→ {{cite web | first=John | last=Doe | coauthors=Peter Smith, Jim Smythe | title=My Favorite Things Part II | publisher=Open Publishing | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2006-05-16 }} ;No author * {{Cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | publisher=Open Publishing | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2006-05-16 }}
→ {{cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | publisher=Open Publishing | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2006-05-16 }} ;No author, no publisher * {{Cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | date=2005-04-30 | work=Encyclopedia of Things | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }} * {{Cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }}
→ {{cite web | title=My Favorite Things Part II | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.example.org/ | accessdate=2005-07-06 }} * {{Cite web | title=List of psychotropic substances under international control | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.incb.org/pdf/e/list/green.pdf | format = PDF | accessdate=2005-07-06 | language=Greek }}
→ {{cite web | title=List of psychotropic substances under international control | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.incb.org/pdf/e/list/green.pdf | format = PDF | accessdate=2005-07-06 | language=Greek }} ; Using archiveurl and archivedate to refer to items that went away but are available from an archive site * {{Cite web | title=List of psychotropic substances under international control | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.incb.org/pdf/e/list/green.pdf | format = PDF | accessdate=2005-07-06 | archiveurl=http://www.archive.org/2005-09-11/www.incb.org/pdf/e/list/green.pdf | archivedate=2005-09-11 }}
→ {{cite web | title=List of psychotropic substances under international control | date=2005-04-30 | url=http://www.incb.org/pdf/e/list/green.pdf | format = PDF | accessdate=2005-07-06 | archiveurl=http://www.archive.org/2005-09-11/www.incb.org/pdf/e/list/green.pdf | archivedate=2005-09-11 }} * {{Cite web |url=http://joanjettbadrep.com/cgi-bin/fullStory.cgi?archive=currnews&story=20060405-01shore.htm |title=Interview with Maggie Downs |date=2006-03-31 |publisher=The Desert Sun |archiveurl=http://72.14.207.104/search?q=cache:JAxf4v-pQmgJ:joanjettbadrep.com/cgi-bin/fullStory.cgi%3Farchive%3Dcurrnews%26story%3D20060405-01shore.htm |archivedate=2006-04-26 }}
→ {{cite web |url=http://joanjettbadrep.com/cgi-bin/fullStory.cgi?archive=currnews&story=20060405-01shore.htm |title=Interview with Maggie Downs |date=2006-03-31 |publisher=The Desert Sun |archiveurl=http://72.14.207.104/search?q=cache:JAxf4v-pQmgJ:joanjettbadrep.com/cgi-bin/fullStory.cgi%3Farchive%3Dcurrnews%26story%3D20060405-01shore.htm |archivedate=2006-04-26}} ; Using quote * {{Cite web | title = My Favorite Things Part II | work = Encyclopedia of Things | url = http://www.example.org/ | accessdate = 2005-07-06 | quote = Lorem ipsum dolor. }}
→ {{cite web | title = My Favorite Things Part II | work = Encyclopedia of Things | url = http://www.example.org/ | accessdate = 2005-07-06 | quote = Lorem ipsum dolor. }} ;No parameters (error) * {{Cite web}}
→ {{cite web}} [[Category:General wiki templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.text: ;Description '''Result:'''  •  It works similarly to the HTML markup sequence:  • , that is, a non-breaking space, a bullet and another non-breaking space. This template is used when you want a larger bullet than a bold middot '''·''', but something smaller than an en dash '''–'''. [[Category:General wiki templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text: ;Description Edit this template to replace the name of the game publisher in the [[Template:Games|Games copyright template]]. If you wish to also link to the publisher's site, you can specify the site url as follows: ;Syntax :{{T|Developer|https://game.site.com}} ;Result :{{Developer|https://game.site.com}} :Alternatively, you can simply remove the logic and hardcode the url in the template, rather than using the unnamed variable: {{{1}}}. This will link to the publisher's site in all instances, rather than optionally.[[Category:Templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.text: <includeonly><infobox type="Item"> <title source="title"/> <image source="image"><caption source="imagecaption"/></image> <data source="type"><label>Type</label></data> <data source="effects"><label>Effects</label></data> <data source="source"><label>Source</label></data> <data source="buy"><label>Cost to buy</label></data> <data source="sell"><label>Cost to sell</label></data> </infobox>{{#ifeq: {{NAMESPACENUMBER}} | 0 | [[Category:Items]]}}</includeonly><noinclude>{{Documentation}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
== Description == To use this template, add the {{T|{{BASEPAGENAME}}}} template and fill in the appropriate fields. Fields left blank will not appear in articles. This infobox template uses [[w:Help:Infobox|Fandom's infobox syntax]]. == Syntax == 
{{Item
 | title         = 
 | image         = [e.g. "Example.jpg"]
 | imagecaption  = 
 | type          = 
 | effects       = 
 | source        = 
 | buy           = 
 | sell          = 
}}
== Sample output == {{Item | title = Power Flower | image = Example.jpg | type = Food | effects = +1 Fire | source = Vendor | buy = 5g | sell = 1g }}
{{Item
 | title         = Power Flower
 | image         = Example.jpg
 | type          = Food
 | effects       = +1 Fire
 | source        = Vendor
 | buy           = 5g
 | sell          = 1g
}}
[[Category:Infobox templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
== Description == To use this template, add the {{T|{{BASEPAGENAME}}}} template and fill in the appropriate fields. Fields left blank will not appear in articles. This infobox template uses [[w:Help:Infobox|Fandom's infobox syntax]]. == Syntax == 
{{Location
 | title         = 
 | image         = [e.g. "Example.jpg"]
 | imagecaption  = 
 | map           = [e.g. "Example.jpg"]
 | mapcaption    = 
 | type          = 
 | level         = 
 | location      = 
 | inhabitants   = 
}}
== Sample output == {{Location | title = Flora Island | image = Example.jpg | imagecaption = The island | map = Example.jpg | mapcaption = Zone map | type = Contested | level = 1-5 | location = Earth | inhabitants = Stompy Humans, Drinky Bees }}
{{Location
 | title         = Flora Island
 | image         = Example.jpg
 | imagecaption  = The island
 | map           = Example.jpg
 | mapcaption    = Zone map
 | type          = Contested
 | level         = 1-5
 | location      = Earth
 | inhabitants   = Stompy Humans, Drinky Bees
}}
[[Category:Infobox templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.text: {| style="width:100%; margin-top:1em; border:1px solid #999; font-size:90%; text-align:center;" |- ! style="padding:0.2em 0.5em;" nowrap="nowrap" class="color1" | {{{header}}} |- | style="padding:0.2em 0.5em;" | {{{body}}} |}<noinclude> {{documentation}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
;Description :This template is used to create a basic navigation box. You can do so by calling the template, via the steps under "Syntax", but it is recommended to '''copy the code verbatim''' via the steps under "Navbox Creation". ;Navbox Creation type=create prefix=Template: preload=Template:Navbox editintro=Template:Navbox/doc buttonlabel=Make your navbox! default = Navbox Foo #Think of a name for your navbox, like "Navbox Foo". Type it in the above field, press the button, and save the page immediately. Be ready to return to ''this'' page to see the rest of the instructions. #Edit the resulting page in source mode. #Replace {{{header}}} with the text you would like to appear in the header. #Replace {{{body}}} with the text you would like to appear in the body. #To add another section, copy these four lines of code immediately below the lines in the existing code that they resemble: 
|-
! style="padding:0.2em 0.5em;" nowrap="nowrap" class="color1" | {{{header}}}
|-
| style="padding:0.2em 0.5em;" | {{{body}}}
Save the page once you have added as many sections as you needed, and filled them with content. You may also want to create a /doc subpage explaining that to call the resulting template, one must only type {{Navbox Foo}}, or rather, whatever we decided to name the template in step 1. ;Syntax 
{{navbox
|header=Land of Bob
|body=This [[place]] and that [[place]].
}}
:Results in... {{navbox |header=Land of Bob |body=This [[place]] and that [[place]]. }} [[Category:Navbox templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.text: <includeonly><infobox type="Quest"> <title source="title"/> <image source="image"><caption source="imagecaption"/></image> <data source="start"><label>Start</label></data> <data source="end"><label>End</label></data> <data source="prerequisites"><label>Prerequisites</label></data> <data source="level"><label>Level</label></data> <data source="location"><label>Location</label></data> <data source="rewards"><label>Rewards</label></data> <group layout="horizontal"> <header>Quest progression</header> <data source="previous"><label>Previous</label></data> <data source="next"><label>Next</label></data> </group> </infobox>{{#ifeq: {{NAMESPACENUMBER}} | 0 | [[Category:Quests]]}}</includeonly><noinclude>{{Documentation}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
== Description == To use this template, add the {{T|{{BASEPAGENAME}}}} template and fill in the appropriate fields. Fields left blank will not appear in articles. This infobox template uses [[w:Help:Infobox|Fandom's infobox syntax]]. == Syntax == 
{{Quest
 | title         = 
 | image         = [e.g. "Example.jpg"]
 | imagecaption  = 
 | start         = 
 | end           = 
 | prerequisites = 
 | level         = 
 | location      = 
 | rewards       = 
 | previous      =
 | next          =
}}
== Sample output == {{Quest | title = Example infobox | image = Example.jpg | start = Chrys | end = Geran | prerequisites = None | level = 5 | location = Earth | rewards = Justice-Wrought Petals | previous = A | next = C }} 
{{Quest
 | title         = Example infobox
 | image         = Example.jpg
 | start         = Chrys
 | end           = Geran
 | prerequisites = None
 | level         = 5
 | location      = Earth
 | rewards       = Justice-Wrought Petals
 | previous      = A
 | next          = C
}}
[[Category:Infobox templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.text: ;Description :This template is used to mark images as being copyrighted, but the copyright holder has given permission for its use. ;Syntax :Type {{t|permission}} on the image information page. [[Category:Image license templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
==Description== To use this template, enter the following and fill in the appropriate fields. Most fields left blank will not show up. ==Syntax== 
{{Quote
 | quote   = 
 | speaker = 
 | source  = 
}}
As an alternative, these can be placed in positional order. ==Samples== {{Quote | quote = When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die. | speaker = [[w:c:gameofthrones:Cersei Lannister|Cersei Lannister]] | source = [[w:c:gameofthrones:You Win or You Die|"You Win or You Die"]] }} 
{{Quote
 | quote   = When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die.
 | speaker = [[w:c:gameofthrones:Cersei Lannister|Cersei Lannister]]
 | source  = [[w:c:gameofthrones:You Win or You Die|"You Win or You Die"]]
}}
or 
{{Quote
 | When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die.
 | [[w:c:gameofthrones:Cersei Lannister|Cersei Lannister]]
 | [[w:c:gameofthrones:You Win or You Die|"You Win or You Die"]]
}}
== Technical details == This template uses the [[w:Help:Lua|Lua templating language]], and more information can be found [[w:c:dev:Global_Lua_Modules/Quote|on the Global Lua Module page]]. '''For a traditional wikitext version of this template, see [[w:c:templates:Template:Quote|Quote on Templates Wiki]]'''. [[Category:Quote templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
This is the {{t|Namespace}} meta-template. It helps other templates detect what type of page they are on. It detects and groups all the different [[Wikipedia:Namespace#Enumeration|namespaces]] used on Fandom into several types: ; main : Main (i.e. article) space, where normal articles are kept. ; talk : Any talk space, including page names that start with "Talk:", "User talk:", "File talk:", etc. ;user ;file ;mediawiki ;template ;help ;category : The remaining namespaces. ; other : Any namespaces that were not specified as a parameter to the template (see explanation below). For backwards compatibility, this template handles '''image''' as if '''file'''. '''image''' ([[Image:...]]) is now deprecated. '''Note:''' For most cases it may be better to use the simpler namespace detection templates (see the [[#See also|see also]] section below). This template is more prone to human errors such as misspelling parameter names. This template uses the [[w:Help:Lua|Lua templating language]], and more information can be found [[w:c:dev:Global_Lua_Modules/Namespace_detect|on the Global Lua Module page]]. '''For a traditional wikitext version of this template, see [[w:c:templates:Template:Namespace_detect|Namespace_detect on Templates Wiki]]'''. == Usage == This template takes one or more parameters named after the different page types as listed above. Like this: 
{{Namespace
 | main  = Article text
 | talk  = Talk page text
 | other = Other pages text
}}
If the template is on a main (article) page, it will return this: : {{Namespace |demospace=main | main = Article text | talk = Talk page text | other = Other pages text }} If the template is on any other page than an article or a talk page, it will return this: : {{Namespace | main = Article text | talk = Talk page text | other = Other pages text }} The example above made the template return something for all page types. But if we don't use the '''other''' parameter or leave it empty, it will not return anything for the other page types. Like this: 
{{Namespace
 | file     = File page text
 | category = Category page text
 | other    =
}}
On any pages other than file and category pages the code above will render nothing. : {{Namespace | file = File page text | category = Category page text | other = }} By using an empty parameter, you can make it so the template doesn't render anything for some specific page type. Like this: 
{{Namespace
 | main  = 
 | other = Other pages text
}}
The code above will render nothing when on mainspace (article) pages, but will return this when on other pages: : {{Namespace | main = | other = Other pages text }} == Demospace and page == For testing and demonstration purposes, this template can take two parameters named '''demospace''' and '''page'''. '''demospace''' understands any of the page type names used by this template, including the '''other''' type. It tells the template to behave like it is on some specific type of page. Like this: 
{{Namespace
 | main  = Article text
 | other = Other pages text
 | demospace = main
}}
No matter on what kind of page the code above is used, it will return this: : {{Namespace | main = Article text | other = Other pages text | demospace = main }} The '''page''' parameter instead takes a normal pagename, making this template behave exactly as if on that page. The pagename doesn't have to be an existing page. Like this: 
{{Namespace
 | user  = User page text
 | other = Other pages text
 | page  = User:Example
}}
No matter on what kind of page the code above is used, it will return this: : {{Namespace | user = User page text | other = Other pages text | page = User:Example }} It can be convenient to let your template understand the '''demospace''' and/or '''page''' parameter and send it on to the {{T|Namespace}} template. Like this: 
{{Namespace
 | main  = Article text
 | other = Other pages text
 | demospace = {{{demospace|}}}
 | page  = {{{page|}}}
}}
If both the '''demospace''' and '''page''' parameters are empty or undefined, the template will detect page types as usual. == Parameters == List of all parameters: 
{{Namespace
| main  = 
...
| other =
| demospace = {{{demospace|}}} / main / talk / user /
              file / mediawiki / template /
              help / category / other
| page  = {{{page|}}} / User:Example
}}
== Technical details == If you intend to feed tables as content to the numbered parameters of this template, you need to know this: [[w:Help:Template|Templates]] have a problem handling parameter data that contains pipes "|" unless the pipe is inside another template {{name|param1}} or inside a piped link [[w:Help:Template|help]]. Thus templates can not handle [[w:Help:Table|wikitables]] as input unless you escape them by using the {{!}} [[w:Help:Magic words|magic word]]. This makes it hard to use wikitables as parameters to templates. Instead, the usual solution is to use HTML wikimarkup for the table code, which is more robust. [[Category:General wiki templates]]{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox|[[Category:Namespace manipulation templates]]}}[[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
== Description == To use this template, add the {{T|{{BASEPAGENAME}}}} template and fill in the appropriate fields. Fields left blank will not appear in articles. This infobox template uses [[w:Help:Infobox|Fandom's infobox syntax]]. == Syntax == 
{{Game
 | title         = The game's name, displayed on top of the infobox
 | image         = [e.g. "Example.jpg"]
 | developer     = 
 | publisher     = 
 | engine        = The engine the game runs on
 | version       = Most recently released version of the game. If your wiki does patch notes, you may wish to link to those.
 | platform      = Platform(s) the game is available on
 | releasedate   = When the game released
 | genre         = Genre(s) the game is classified as
 | mode          = Singeplayer, multiplayer, etc.
 | rating        = ESRB / PEGI, etc. ratings
 | media         = What media the game is available on (digital, cartridge, cd, etc.)
 | requirements  = System requirements
}}
== Sample output == {{Game | title = The Game of Flower | image = Example.jpg | developer = | publisher = | engine = | version = | platform = PC | releasedate = Soon | genre = RPG | mode = Single player | rating = | media = | requirements = Cutting edge hardware }} 
{{Game
 | title         = The Game of Flower
 | image         = Example.jpg
 | developer     = 
 | publisher     = 
 | engine        = 
 | version       = 
 | platform      = PC
 | releasedate   = Soon
 | genre         = RPG
 | mode          = Single player
 | rating        = 
 | media         = 
 | requirements  = Cutting edge hardware
}}
[[Category:Infobox templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.text: ;Description :This template is used to create the box used by the various image license templates. The default styling is currently geared to a light-themed wiki. If your wiki has a dark theme and this template is too bright relative to the other elements on your wiki, simply change the following style parameters: :background-color: This is the color of the background and is currently set to: #fefefe :border-color: This is the color of the borders and is currently set to: #d6d6d6 :color: This is the color of the text and is currently set to: #333 ;Syntax :Type {{t|LicenseBox|text{{=}}License text}} on the image information page. [[Category:Image license templates| ]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: Updated default StructuredQuote template. See https://community.fandom.com/wiki/Help:Structured_Quotes for more details.
revision.text:
{{{text|Text...}}}

—{{{speaker|speaker}}}{{#if:{{{receiver|}}}|, to {{{receiver|}}}}}{{#if:{{{attribution|}}}|, {{{attribution|}}}}}{{#if:{{{source|}}}|, {{{source|}}}}}

==Description== A template used for displaying Structured Quotes ( ''{{#SQuote:}}'' ). If you want to unlock the full potential of Structured Quotes, please avoid using this template directly, and consider ''{{#SQuote:}}'' markup instead. See https://community.fandom.com/wiki/Help:Structured_Quotes for extra information about Structured Quotes. ==Syntax== 
{{StructuredQuote
| text   =
| speaker =
| receiver =
| attribution =
| source  =
}}
==Samples== {{StructuredQuote |text=Size matters not. Look at me. Judge me by my size, do you? Hmm? Hmm. And well you should not. (...) |speaker=[[w:c:en.starwars:Yoda|Yoda]] |receiver=[[w:c:en.starwars:Luke_Skywalker|Luke Skywalker]] |source=[[w:c:en.starwars:Star_Wars:_Episode_V_The_Empire_Strikes_Back|Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back]] }} 
{{StructuredQuote
|text=Size matters not. Look at me. Judge me by my size, do you? Hmm? Hmm. And well you should not. (...)
|speaker=[[Yoda]]
|receiver=[[Luke Skywalker]]
|source=[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back]]
}}
== TemplateData == { "params": { "text": { "label": "Quote text", "description": "quote text", "type": "content", "required": true }, "speaker": { "label": "Person(s) quoted", "description": "individual(s) who uttered or wrote the quoted words (wikitext links, comma-separated)", "type": "content", "required": true }, "receiver": { "label": "Person(s) to whom the quote was spoken", "description": "person the quote was spoken to (wikitext links, comma-separated)", "type": "content", "suggested": true }, "attribution": { "label": "Attribution(s)", "description": "attribution (wikitext links, comma-separated)", "type": "content", "suggested": true }, "source": { "label": "Quote source", "description": "place where it was spoken (wikitext link)", "type": "content", "suggested": true } }, "format": "block" }

revision.text: ;Description Edit this template to replace the name of the game publisher in the [[Template:Games|Games copyright template]]. If you wish to also link to the publisher's site, you can specify the site url as follows: ;Syntax :{{T|Publisher|https://game.site.com}} ;Result :{{Publisher|https://game.site.com}} :Alternatively, you can simply remove the logic and hardcode the url in the template, rather than using the unnamed variable: {{{1}}}. This will link to the publisher's site in all instances, rather than optionally.[[Category:Templates]][[Category:Template documentation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
== Description == This template uses your wiki's SITENAME and strips off the word "wiki" leaving what is in most cases the text describing the topic of your wiki. To use this template in its basic form, simply add the {{T|{{BASEPAGENAME}}}} template wherever you want your topic to appear in a sentence or section. If your wiki's SITENAME minus the word "wiki" is not a good descriptor for your wiki's topic, you may also simply edit the template and replace it with just the text you want displayed. == Syntax == 
{{Topic}}
== Sample output == {{Topic}} [[Category:Templates]][[Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
-- This Module is used for making templates based in the Lua language. -- See more details about Lua in [[w:Help:Lua]]. -- The Fandom Developer's Wiki hosts Global Lua Modules that can be imported and locally overridden. -- The next line imports the Dialogue module from the [[w:c:dev:Global Lua Modules]]. local Dialogue = require('Dev:Dialogue') -- See more details about this module at [[w:c:dev:Global_Lua_Modules/Dialogue]]
-- The last line produces the output for the template return Dialogue

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
-- This Module is used for making templates based in the Lua language. -- See more details about Lua in [[w:Help:Lua]]. -- The Fandom Developer's Wiki hosts Global Lua Modules that can be imported and locally overridden. -- The next line imports the Hatnote module from the [[w:c:dev:Global Lua Modules]]. local H = require('Dev:Hatnote') -- See more details about this module at [[w:c:dev:Global_Lua_Modules/Hatnote]]
-- The last line produces the output for the template return H

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Restore of Underground wiki.
revision.text:
-- This Module is used for making templates based in the Lua language. -- See more details about Lua in [[w:Help:Lua]]. -- The Fandom Developer's Wiki hosts Global Lua Modules that can be imported and locally overridden. -- The next line imports the Mbox module from the [[w:c:dev:Global Lua Modules]]. local Mbox = require('Dev:Mbox') -- See more details about this module at [[w:c:dev:Global_Lua_Modules/Mbox]]
-- The imported Module is overwritten locally to include default styling. -- For a more flexible Mbox experience, delete the function below and import -- https://dev.fandom.com/wiki/MediaWiki:Global_Lua_Modules/Mbox.css -- or paste (and modify as you like) its contents in your wiki's -- [[MediaWiki:Wikia.css]] (see [[w:Help:Including_additional_CSS_and_JS]]) -- or look at https://dev.fandom.com/wiki/Global_Lua_Modules/Mbox -- for more customization inspiration
-- -- BEGIN DELETION HERE --
local getArgs = require('Dev:Arguments').getArgs local localCSS = mw.loadData('Module:Mbox/data').localStyle
function Mbox.main(frame) local args = getArgs(frame)
-- styles local styles = {} if args.bordercolor then styles['border-left-color'] = args.bordercolor elseif args.type then styles['border-left-color'] = 'var(--type-' .. args.type .. ')' end
if args.bgcolor then styles['background-color'] = args.bgcolor end
-- images local image = args.image or '' local imagewidth = args.imagewidth or '80px' local imagelink = '' if args.imagelink then imagelink = '|link=' .. args.imagelink end
local imagewikitext = ('%sFile:%s|%s%s' .. ']]'):format('[[', image, imagewidth, imagelink)
-- id for closure local id = args.id or 'mbox'
local container = mw.html.create('div') :addClass('mbox') :addClass(args.class) :css(styles) :css(localCSS['mbox']) :cssText(args.style)
local content = container:tag('div') :addClass('mbox__content') :css(localCSS['mbox__content'])
if args.image then local image = content:tag('div') :addClass('mbox__content__image') :addClass('mw-collapsible') :attr('id', 'mw-customcollapsible-' .. id) :css(localCSS['mbox__content__image']) :wikitext(imagewikitext) if args.collapsed then image:addClass('mw-collapsed') end end
local contentwrapper = content:tag('div') :addClass('mbox__content__wrapper') :css(localCSS['mbox__content__wrapper'])
if args.header then contentwrapper:tag('div') :addClass('mbox__content__header') :css(localCSS['mbox__content__header']) :wikitext(args.header) end
if args.text then local text = contentwrapper:tag('div') :addClass('mbox__content__text') :addClass('mw-collapsible') :attr('id', 'mw-customcollapsible-' .. id) :css(localCSS['mbox__content__text']) :wikitext(args.text) if args.collapsed then text:addClass('mw-collapsed') end
if args.comment then text:tag('div') :addClass('mbox__content__text__comment') :css(localCSS['mbox__content__text__comment']) :wikitext(args.comment) end end
contentwrapper:tag('span') :addClass('mbox__close') :addClass('mw-customtoggle-' .. id) :css(localCSS['mbox__close']) :attr('title', 'Dismiss')
if args.aside then local aside = content:tag('div') :addClass('mbox__content__aside') :addClass('mw-collapsible') :attr('id', 'mw-customcollapsible-' .. id) :css(localCSS['mbox__content__aside']) :wikitext(args.aside) if args.collapsed then aside:addClass('mw-collapsed') end end
return container end
-- -- END DELETION HERE --
-- The last line produces the output for the template return Mbox
